entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop being studied for disease resistance to early leaf spot

entities.name: EARLY LEAF SPOT
entities.description: A major biotic constraint affecting groundnut production caused by Cercospora arachidicola

entities.name: WEST AND CENTRAL AFRICA
entities.description: The region where early leaf spot is a major constraint for groundnut production

entities.name: CERCOSPORA ARACHIDICOLA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes early leaf spot disease in groundnut

entities.name: DIALLEL ANALYSIS
entities.description: A breeding method using F2 full diallel populations to assess the mode of inheritance of traits

entities.name: ELS RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of groundnut to resist early leaf spot disease

entities.name: RANDOMIZED COMPLETE BLOCK DESIGN
entities.description: The experimental design used to grow the F2 populations and parents

entities.name: ELS DISEASE SEVERITY
entities.description: A measure of the extent of early leaf spot disease on the plant

entities.name: AREA UNDER DISEASE PROGRESS CURVE
entities.description: A method used to estimate the progress of the early leaf spot disease

entities.name: ADDITIVE GENE ACTION
entities.description: A type of gene action found to be predominant in the inheritance of ELS resistance

entities.name: NON-ADDITIVE GENE ACTION
entities.description: A type of gene action involved in the inheritance of ELS resistance

entities.name: RECIPROCAL CROSS EFFECT
entities.description: An effect observed in crosses suggesting a cytoplasmic influence on ELS resistance

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC EFFECT
entities.description: The influence of the cytoplasm on the inheritance of ELS resistance

entities.name: GRAPHICAL ANALYSIS
entities.description: A method used to reveal the predominance of additive gene action for ELS resistance

entities.name: EARLY GENERATION SELECTION
entities.description: A selection strategy suggested to be effective for improving ELS resistance

entities.description: A parental groundnut line identified as a good donor for ELS resistance
